{"key_event":"Jacksonian democracy and Indian Removal","reasoning":"Mass electoral parties, partisan presses, rallies, and reform associations strengthened organized civil society among white citizens, but gains were sharply offset by proslavery repression and the destruction of Native and Black collective autonomy. State power rose as the presidency, federal sovereignty, and settler/slave-state coercion expanded, though the Bank War and decentralization of finance kept overall state capacity only moderate.","society_change":0.2,"society_power":7.5,"state_change":0.4,"state_power":6.2}
